description: v2 i64 kernel exports per wasm-abi.md §10. Covers all four families (elementwise §10.2, reductions §10.3, selection §10.4, hash §10.4). i64 data values are encoded as decimal STRINGS in JSON (e.g. '9223372036854775807'); the runner packs them into BigInt64Array. Scalar BigInt parameters (add_i64_scalar 's', fill_null_i64 'fill') are also decimal strings. Reduction results returned as i64 (sum/min/max/first/last) are decimal strings; f64-returning reductions (mean/std/var) are JSON numbers. Validity bitmaps ('*_vp' keys) follow the same 0/1 integer array convention as v1 (README §2.2). Wrapping arithmetic (dtypes.md §6): add/sub/mul/neg wrap mod 2^64 (two's complement), no trap. div/mod zero divisor: kernel must not trap; output slot value is unspecified (JSON null); caller's expression compiler signals null via validity. cast semantics: dtypes.md §7.1.

cases.name: add_i64__len5
cases.note: len=5 is not a multiple of 8 — exercises scalar tail

cases.name: add_i64__wrap_max_plus_one
cases.note: INT64_MAX + 1 wraps to INT64_MIN (two's complement mod 2^64, dtypes.md §6)

cases.name: add_i64__wrap_min_minus_one
cases.note: INT64_MIN + (-1) wraps to INT64_MAX

cases.name: sub_i64__wrap_min_sub_one
cases.note: INT64_MIN - 1 wraps to INT64_MAX (dtypes.md §6)

cases.name: mul_i64__wrap_max_times_two
cases.note: INT64_MAX * 2 = 2^64 - 2 wraps to -2 (two's complement)

cases.name: div_i64__truncate_toward_zero
cases.note: Truncation toward zero (not floor): 7/2=3, -7/2=-3, 7/-2=-3, -7/-2=3

cases.name: div_i64__zero_divisor_no_trap
cases.note: Zero divisor must NOT trap (wasm-abi.md §7). Kernel writes 0 (or any value) to that slot. The JS expression compiler, not the kernel, signals null via out_vp using ne_i64_scalar_mask on divisor.

cases.name: div_i64__min_div_neg1_wraps
cases.note: i64::MIN / -1 overflows i64: the mathematical result 2^63 exceeds i64::MAX (= 2^63-1). Rust wrapping_div returns i64::MIN because 2^63 interpreted as a signed 64-bit two's-complement value has bit pattern 0x8000000000000000 = -2^63 = i64::MIN. NOTE: the verifier corrected an earlier note — 'i64::MIN + i64::MIN in two's complement = INT64_MIN' was wrong; wrapping_add(I64_MIN, I64_MIN) = wrap64(-2^64) = 0, not I64_MIN. The correct explanation is the bit-pattern reinterpretation of the overflowed result 2^63 in signed i64. Guarded, no trap (ADR-009).

cases.name: mod_i64__truncated_remainder
cases.note: Result sign follows dividend (truncated-division semantics, ADR-009)

cases.name: mod_i64__zero_divisor_no_trap
cases.note: Zero divisor must not trap; kernel writes 0 (or any value). Same pattern as div_i64.

cases.name: add_i64_scalar__basic
cases.note: Scalar 's' is a BigInt in JS (wasm-abi.md §10.1); encoded as decimal string in fixture

cases.name: add_i64_scalar__wrap
cases.note: INT64_MAX + 1n wraps to INT64_MIN

cases.name: mul_i64_scalar__86400000
cases.note: date32->timestamp scale cast lowers to mul_i64_scalar(86_400_000) (wasm-abi.md §11)

cases.name: div_i64_scalar__zero_scalar
cases.note: All elements yield null (unspecified output) when scalar is 0

cases.name: neg_i64__wrap_min
cases.note: neg(INT64_MIN) = -(-2^63) = 2^63, which wraps back to INT64_MIN (no trap)

cases.name: neg_i64__max
cases.note: neg(INT64_MAX) = -9223372036854775807 (exact, no wrap)

cases.name: gt_i64_mask__boundary
cases.note: INT64_MIN < INT64_MAX; 0 > -1

cases.name: ne_i64_mask__len9_bitmap_padding
cases.note: len=9 requires 2 bitmap bytes; padding bits 9-15 in byte 2 must be 0

cases.name: gt_i64_scalar_mask__basic
cases.note: Scalar 's' is BigInt in JS; decimal string in fixture

cases.name: ne_i64_scalar_mask__zero_divisor_check_pattern
cases.note: ne_i64_scalar_mask(divisor_col, 0n) is how the expression compiler detects zero divisors for div/mod null signaling

cases.name: cast_f64_i64__basic_trunc_toward_zero
cases.note: f64->i64: truncate toward zero (same failure model as f64->i32, dtypes.md §7.1). out_vp set by kernel for range/NaN failures.

cases.name: cast_f64_i64__range_null
cases.note: Inputs where |x| >= 2^63 or x is NaN/±Inf produce null (out_vp=0), never trap. 9.9e18 > 2^63 = 9.22e18. Two in-range boundary values: below2_63 = 9223372036854773760.0 (= 2^63 - 2048, two ULPs below 2^63) and prev2_63 = 9223372036854774784.0 (= 2^63 - 1024 = prevDouble(2^63); ULP in [2^62,2^63) is 1024 so this is one ULP below 2^63, bit pattern 0x43DFFFFFFFFFFFFF). Both are strictly < 2^63 and must produce VALID outputs. NOTE: the verifier corrected an earlier note — prevDouble(2^63) is 2^63-1024 NOT 2^63-2048; including prev2_63 closes the boundary-off-by-ULP coverage gap.

cases.name: cast_f64_i64__null_propagation
cases.note: A null input (in_vp=0) produces a null output regardless of data value

cases.name: cast_f64_i64__no_null_fast_path
cases.note: Omit in_vp key to test all-valid fast path (in_vp pointer = 0)

cases.name: cast_f32_i64__basic_trunc_toward_zero
cases.note: f32->i64: same semantics as f64->i64 after the f32->f64 widening

cases.name: cast_f32_i64__range_null
cases.note: NaN and ±Infinity -> null; large f32 values also -> null

cases.name: cast_i32_i64__sign_extend
cases.note: i32->i64: exact sign-extend; negative i32 gets sign-extended to full i64 (dtypes.md §7.1 '=')

cases.name: cast_u32_i64__zero_extend
cases.note: u32->i64: exact zero-extend; u32 max = 4294967295 zero-extends to 4294967295n (not -1) (dtypes.md §7.1 '=')

cases.name: cast_bool_i64__basic
cases.note: bool->i64: true->1n, false->0n (dtypes.md §7.1)

cases.name: cast_i64_f64__exact_range
cases.note: i64->f64: exact when |x| <= 2^53 = 9007199254740992; NEVER null (dtypes.md §7.1 '≈'). Zero, small positive/negative, and the boundary 2^53 are exact.

cases.name: cast_i64_f64__rounds_above_2_53
cases.note: i64->f64 at 2^53+1 = 9007199254740993: rounds to 2^53 = 9007199254740992.0 (round-to-nearest-even; equidistant from 2^53 and 2^53+2; 2^53 has even significand). Row stays VALID — widening casts never null (dtypes.md §7.1). Working: f64 ULP at [2^53,2^54) is 2; 9007199254740993 midpoint rounds to 9007199254740992 (even).

cases.name: cast_i64_f64__boundary_values
cases.note: INT64_MAX = 9223372036854775807 rounds to 9223372036854775808 = 2^63 (distance to 2^63 is 1; distance to prevDouble(2^63) = 2^63-1024 = 9223372036854774784 is 1023; rounds UP to 2^63). INT64_MIN = -2^63 is exactly representable as f64. Both stay valid. NOTE: the verifier corrected an earlier note — the adjacent lower f64 is 2^63-1024 (ULP in [2^62,2^63) is 1024), so the distance to it is 1023 not 2047. The conclusion (rounds to 2^63) and expected output are correct.

cases.name: cast_i64_f32__exact_range
cases.note: i64->f32: exact when |x| <= 2^24 = 16777216 (f32 has 24-bit significand including implicit 1). Values above 2^24 may round. NEVER null.

cases.name: cast_i64_f32__rounds_above_2_24
cases.note: 16777217 = 2^24+1: f32 ULP at [2^24,2^25) is 2; 16777217 equidistant from 16777216 and 16777218; round-to-even -> 16777216 (even). 16777219 = 2^24+3: equidistant from 16777218 and 16777220; round-to-even -> 16777220 (even). Both stay valid.

cases.name: cast_i64_i32__wrap_truncate
cases.note: i64->i32: keep low 32 bits, signed. NEVER null (dtypes.md §7.1 'wrap-truncate'). 0x1_0000_0001 = 2^32+1 -> low32 = 1. INT64_MAX = 0x7FFFFFFF_FFFFFFFF -> low32 = 0xFFFFFFFF = -1 signed.

cases.name: cast_i64_u32__wrap_truncate
cases.note: i64->u32: keep low 32 bits, unsigned. NEVER null. -1n -> 0xFFFFFFFF = 4294967295. INT64_MAX = 0x7FFFFFFF_FFFFFFFF -> low32 = 0xFFFFFFFF = 4294967295. 2^32+1 -> 1.

cases.name: cast_i64_bool__basic
cases.note: i64->bool: x!=0 -> 1, 0 -> 0; NEVER null for valid input (dtypes.md §7.1)

cases.name: fill_null_i64__basic
cases.note: fill_null_i64: replace null slots with fill value; output is all-valid. 'fill' is BigInt in JS, decimal string in fixture (wasm-abi.md §10.2).

cases.name: fill_null_i64__no_nulls
cases.note: No nulls: output unchanged (all-valid fast path)

cases.name: sum_i64_null__empty
cases.note: Empty sum = 0n (additive identity, wasm-abi.md §10.7). Kernel returns i64 -> JS receives bigint.

cases.name: sum_i64_null__no_null_fast_path
cases.note: Omit vp key -> validity_ptr=0 (all-valid fast path)

cases.name: sum_i64_null__mixed_skipna
cases.note: Nulls are skipped; only valid values contribute (skipna semantics, dtypes.md §4.3)

cases.name: sum_i64_null__all_null
cases.note: All-null sum = 0n (additive identity, dtypes.md §4.3). API layer returns 0n even when all-null (no null sentinel for sum).

cases.name: sum_i64_null__wrapping
cases.note: Wrapping sum: [INT64_MAX, 1, 2] = 9223372036854775807 + 1 + 2 wraps to -9223372036854775806 (two's complement mod 2^64). Order-insensitive: wrapping i64 add is associative and commutative (wasm-abi.md §10.6).

cases.name: sum_i64_null__two_maxes_wrap_to_minus_two
cases.note: INT64_MAX + INT64_MAX = 2*(2^63-1) = 2^64-2, which wraps to -2 in signed i64

cases.name: mean_i64_null__empty
cases.note: mean_i64 of empty/all-null = NaN (f64). wasm-abi.md §10.7. Kernel returns f64 NaN; API maps to null via count_null.

cases.name: mean_i64_null__large_values
cases.note: mean_i64 of [10, 20, 30] = 20.0 (f64, exact since values << 2^53)

cases.name: min_i64_null__empty
cases.note: Empty/all-null: kernel returns 0n (sentinel per wasm-abi.md §10.7); API layer maps to null via count_null.

cases.name: std_i64_null__empty
cases.note: Fewer than 2 non-null values -> NaN (wasm-abi.md §10.7 / dtypes.md §11)

cases.name: std_i64_null__skipna
cases.note: std([1, null, 3]) over non-null [1, 3]: mean=2, sq_dev=(1-2)^2+(3-2)^2=2, var=2/1=2, std=sqrt(2)

cases.name: var_i64_null__precision_above_2_53
cases.note: Same precision case as std above: var([2^53, 2^53+2]) = 4.0 due to mean rounding; exact answer would be 2.0.

cases.name: nunique_i64_null__nulls_not_counted
cases.note: Nulls are not counted as a distinct value (dtypes.md §4.3)

cases.name: first_i64_null__basic
cases.note: first_i64_null returns the first NON-NULL value (skipna). 'out_valid' is 1 when a non-null was found. Return value is i64 -> BigInt in JS -> decimal string in fixture.

cases.name: first_i64_null__all_null
cases.note: All-null: out_valid=0; return value is unspecified (per wasm-abi.md §10.7: value 0n)

cases.name: last_i64_null__basic
cases.note: last_i64_null returns the last NON-NULL value (skipna).

cases.name: filter_i64__empty
cases.note: filter_i64 compacts values where mask bit=1; returns element count

cases.name: filter_i64__len9_mask_padding
cases.note: len=9 uses 2 bitmap bytes; padding bits 9-15 in byte 2 must be 0 and not affect output

cases.name: gather_i64__basic
cases.note: out[k] = data[idx[k]]

cases.name: argsort_i64__empty
cases.note: argsort_i64 signature: (data, vp, inout_perm, len, desc, scratch_ptr)->(); inout_perm is caller-initialized identity for single-key, stably reordered. v1.2 scratch_ptr = caller i32[len].

cases.name: argsort_i64__nulls_last_ascending
cases.note: Nulls sort LAST in ascending order (dtypes.md §4.6). Valid values: 30 (idx 0), 10 (idx 2). Null at idx 1 goes last.

cases.name: argsort_i64__nulls_last_descending
cases.note: Nulls sort LAST even in descending order (dtypes.md §4.6). Valid values: 10 (idx 2), 30 (idx 0). Null at idx 1 goes last.

cases.name: argsort_i64__stability
cases.note: Equal keys preserve original row order (stable sort). Elements at idx 0 and 2 have same value 10; original order must be preserved.

cases.name: argsort_i64__boundary_values
cases.note: Total signed order: INT64_MIN < -1 < 0 < 1 < INT64_MAX

cases.name: argsort_i64__len9_scalar_tail
cases.note: len=9 is not a multiple of 2 (i64x2 SIMD width) — exercises scalar tail in SIMD build

cases.name: topk_i64__empty
cases.note: topk_i64 signature: (data, vp, k, out_idx, len, largest)->i32. Returns count written (min(k, non-null count)). largest=1 -> k largest (desc), largest=0 -> k smallest (asc).

cases.name: topk_i64__excludes_nulls
cases.note: topk excludes null rows (dtypes.md §4.6); null at idx 2 is excluded. k=3 but only 2 valid -> writes 2.

cases.name: topk_i64__ties_lower_index_first
cases.note: Ties: lower original index first (dtypes.md §4.6). Values at idx 0,2 are equal 10; idx 0 is first.

cases.name: hash_i64__equal_values_equal_hashes
cases.note: hash_i64 maps each i64 via splitmix64 finalizer (wasm-abi.md §10.4). Exact hash values are implementation-defined; this is a PROPERTY TEST: equal inputs must produce equal hashes. Verifier: positions that share a data value must produce identical i64 hash outputs.

cases.name: hash_i64__null_rows_hash_to_H_NULL
cases.note: Null rows (vp=0) must hash to H_NULL = 0x9e3779b97f4a7c15 = -7046029254386353131 (signed i64). This is a PROPERTY TEST for the null-hash constant: all null positions must produce the same hash value as each other (and the verifier knows H_NULL).

cases.name: hash_i64__mixed_validity
cases.note: Property test: non-null values with same data get equal hashes; all null positions get H_NULL.

cases.name: hash_i64__boundary_values
cases.note: Property test: INT64_MIN and INT64_MAX have distinct hashes from 0 and each other (splitmix64 avalanche; not a guarantee but a sanity property). Verifier checks equal-value groups.

cases.name: construct_i64__unsafe_int_positive_throws
cases.note: Number 9007199254740992 (= 2^53) fails Number.isSafeInteger because |x| = 2^53 > Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER = 2^53-1. ADR-009 §Decision: 'a number must be an exact integer in the safe-integer range (Number.isSafeInteger); a non-integer or out-of-safe-range number throws a helpful error naming the offending value.' Silent precision loss is worse than a throw. The runner invokes column construction for i64 with a plain-number array containing this value and asserts the throw.

cases.name: construct_i64__unsafe_int_negative_throws
cases.note: Number -9007199254740992 (= -2^53) also fails Number.isSafeInteger (|x| = 2^53 > 2^53-1). Same throw rule as positive case. Error must name the offending value.

cases.name: construct_i64__non_integer_throws
cases.note: Number 1.5 is not an integer (Number.isInteger(1.5) === false). ADR-009 §Decision: a non-integer number throws a helpful error naming the offending value. The runner passes 1.5 as a plain-number element to i64 column construction.

cases.name: construct_i64__nan_throws
cases.note: NaN is a Number but not null/undefined. ADR-009 §Decision: 'a NaN number is not a null here — it is an invalid i64 literal and throws.' The runner passes the JS value NaN (not null) as an element; 'NaN' string in fixtures encodes the Number NaN per README §2.1. Error must mention NaN.
